{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Electrical Engineer (Power Plant)",
  "description": "Paras Energy was incorporated in the year 2002. We are a diversified and internationally recognized energy company that provides stable and reliable power to all customers by generation and distribution of power while maintaining stringent adherence to OEM guidelines of operation and maintenance. Apart from power generation and distribution, we can also provide EPC and maintenance services for substations, transmission line and pressure reducing &amp; measuring stations. At Paras Energy, we believe that electricity should be accessible and affordable to all. Over the years, we have gathered a diverse clientele all over Nigeria and we continue to grow our portfolio through the provision of reliable services. On 8th April 2016, Paras Energy became the 27th Company to supply power to National Grid in Nigeria and the first privately owned company to supply power under a bilateral agreement to their customers.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Electrical Engineer (Power Plant)

Job Location: Ogijo, Ogun

Job Description


	We are seeking experienced Electrical Engineer to join our team at Paras Energy to work within our Power Plant facility at Agbara Location.
	The ideal candidate will be responsible for ensuring the efficient and safe operation of electrical systems, with a focus on 33KV, 11kV VCB panels, breaker maintenance, wiring troubleshooting, maintaining checklists, generator fault troubleshooting, reading wiring diagrams and rectification.
	The candidate will report to the Electrical Head and collaborate closely with other engineering and operational teams. Should have computer skills on MS Office and email writings for communication and reporting.


Key Responsibilities


	Perform routine inspection, maintenance, and troubleshooting of 33kv, 11kV VCB panels, ACB, VCB, transformers, cables, and other electrical equipment within the power plant.
	Conduct overhead line patrols to identify and address any potential electrical issues or hazards.
	Develop and maintain checklists for regular equipment inspections and maintenance activities.
	Execute breaker maintenance procedures, including testing, servicing, and repairs as needed.
	Troubleshoot generator issues, diagnose faults, and implement appropriate rectification measures to minimize downtime.
	Collaborate with maintenance and operations teams to ensure timely resolution of electrical issues and minimize disruptions to power plant operations.
	Responsible for Power plant operation and maintenance.
	Adhere to safety protocols and regulations at all times, promoting a culture of safety within the workplace.
	Keep accurate records of operational parameters, maintenance activities, equipment status, and performance metrics.
	Demonstrated ability to perform maintenance, troubleshooting, and repair tasks effectively and efficiently.
	Strong knowledge of Medium voltage systems, Low Voltage system, transformers, ACB, VCB, CT, PT, Energy meters, Starters, VFD, Cable Termination and jointings and related electrical components.


Qualifications and Skills


	H.N.D or Bachelor&#39;s Degree in Electrical Engineering or related field.
	Proven experience working in a power plant or similar industrial environment, with a focus on electrical systems and equipment.
	Familiarity with standard safety practices and regulations governing electrical systems and equipment.
	Excellent problem-solving skills with the ability to diagnose and rectify electrical faults promptly.
	Knowledge of computer software&rsquo;s and reporting tools, email, and MS office
	Effective communication and interpersonal skills, with the ability to collaborate effectively with cross-functional teams.
	Detail-oriented with strong organizational skills and the ability to manage multiple tasks simultaneously.
	Ideal Candidates will be resident in Agbara, Ogun State or willing to relocate to Agbaraat their own expense.
	Be Innovative, creative, hardworking, resilient, and self-motivated
	Ability to work on shifts including Saturdays.


Method of Application
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: careers@Parasenergy.com and copy hr.ogijo@Parasenergy.com using the Job Position and Location as the subject of the mail.",
  "datePosted": "2026-06-01",
  "validThrough": "2026-07-01",

Electrical Engineer (Power Plant) Job at Paras Energy




        
    

        
        
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    

Paras Energy was incorporated in the year 2002. We are a diversified and internationally recognized energy company that provides stable and reliable power to all customers by generation and distribution of power while maintaining stringent adherence to OEM guidelines of operation and maintenance. Apart from power generation and distribution, we can also provide EPC and maintenance services for substations, transmission line and pressure reducing & measuring stations. At Paras Energy, we believe that electricity should be accessible and affordable to all. Over the years, we have gathered a diverse clientele all over Nigeria and we continue to grow our portfolio through the provision of reliable services. On 8th April 2016, Paras Energy became the 27th Company to supply power to National Grid in Nigeria and the first privately owned company to supply power under a bilateral agreement to their customers.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Electrical Engineer (Power Plant)

Job Location: Ogijo, Ogun

Job Description


	We are seeking experienced Electrical Engineer to join our team at Paras Energy to work within our Power Plant facility at Agbara Location.
	The ideal candidate will be responsible for ensuring the efficient and safe operation of electrical systems, with a focus on 33KV, 11kV VCB panels, breaker maintenance, wiring troubleshooting, maintaining checklists, generator fault troubleshooting, reading wiring diagrams and rectification.
	The candidate will report to the Electrical Head and collaborate closely with other engineering and operational teams. Should have computer skills on MS Office and email writings for communication and reporting.


Key Responsibilities


	Perform routine inspection, maintenance, and troubleshooting of 33kv, 11kV VCB panels, ACB, VCB, transformers, cables, and other electrical equipment within the power plant.
	Conduct overhead line patrols to identify and address any potential electrical issues or hazards.
	Develop and maintain checklists for regular equipment inspections and maintenance activities.
	Execute breaker maintenance procedures, including testing, servicing, and repairs as needed.
	Troubleshoot generator issues, diagnose faults, and implement appropriate rectification measures to minimize downtime.
	Collaborate with maintenance and operations teams to ensure timely resolution of electrical issues and minimize disruptions to power plant operations.
	Responsible for Power plant operation and maintenance.
	Adhere to safety protocols and regulations at all times, promoting a culture of safety within the workplace.
	Keep accurate records of operational parameters, maintenance activities, equipment status, and performance metrics.
	Demonstrated ability to perform maintenance, troubleshooting, and repair tasks effectively and efficiently.
	Strong knowledge of Medium voltage systems, Low Voltage system, transformers, ACB, VCB, CT, PT, Energy meters, Starters, VFD, Cable Termination and jointings and related electrical components.


Qualifications and Skills


	H.N.D or Bachelor's Degree in Electrical Engineering or related field.
	Proven experience working in a power plant or similar industrial environment, with a focus on electrical systems and equipment.
	Familiarity with standard safety practices and regulations governing electrical systems and equipment.
	Excellent problem-solving skills with the ability to diagnose and rectify electrical faults promptly.
	Knowledge of computer software’s and reporting tools, email, and MS office
	Effective communication and interpersonal skills, with the ability to collaborate effectively with cross-functional teams.
	Detail-oriented with strong organizational skills and the ability to manage multiple tasks simultaneously.
	Ideal Candidates will be resident in Agbara, Ogun State or willing to relocate to Agbaraat their own expense.
	Be Innovative, creative, hardworking, resilient, and self-motivated
	Ability to work on shifts including Saturdays.


Method of Application
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] and copy [email protected] using the Job Position and Location as the subject of the

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Accountant",
  "description": "Zizi Dredging and Marine Services is a growing dredging and marine services company based in Calabar, specializing in dredging operations, sand filling, land reclamation, shoreline protection, and marine logistics services.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Accountant

Job Location: Calabar, Cross River State
Employment Type: Full-Time
Industry: Dredging / Marine Operations

Job Summary


	As part of our growth, we are seeking a smart, detail-oriented, and proactive Accountant to join our dredging operations team in Calabar.
	The ideal candidate will be responsible for managing financial records, monitoring operational expenses, preparing reports, and ensuring accurate accounting processes within the company.


Key Responsibilities


	Maintain accurate financial records and accounting documentation.
	Prepare daily, weekly, and monthly financial reports.
	Monitor cash flow, expenses, and operational costs.
	Process invoices, payments, receipts, and bank reconciliations.
	Manage accounts payable and receivable effectively.
	Assist in payroll preparation and statutory deductions.
	Prepare and post journal entries into accounting systems.
	Support budget preparation and expense tracking for dredging operations.
	Ensure compliance with financial policies, procedures, and regulatory requirements.
	Liaise with auditors, banks, and relevant government agencies when required.
	Maintain proper filing and documentation of financial transactions.
	Provide management with accurate financial analysis and recommendations.


Requirements


	Bachelor&rsquo;s Degree or HND in Accounting, Finance, or related discipline.
	2&ndash;3 years of proven experience in an accounting role.
	Experience in construction, marine, logistics, oil &amp; gas, or dredging industry is an added advantage.
	Strong knowledge of accounting principles and financial reporting.
	Proficiency in accounting software such as QuickBooks, Sage, Tally, Zoho Books, or similar ERP/accounting systems.
	Good knowledge of Microsoft Excel and other Microsoft Office applications.
	Ability to work independently and meet deadlines.
	Strong analytical, organizational, and problem-solving skills.
	High level of integrity, accuracy, and attention to detail.
	ICAN/ACCA certification is an advantage but not compulsory.


Preferred Skills:


	Cost control and operational expense monitoring.
	Inventory and asset tracking experience.
	Ability to prepare management reports and financial summaries.
	Good communication and teamwork skills.


Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: dredgingrecruitment2026@gmail.com using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.",

Accountant Job at Zizi Dredging and Marine Services




        
    

        
        Zizi Dredging and Marine Services is a growing dredging and marine services company based in Calabar, specializing in dredging operations, sand filling, land reclamation, shoreline protection, and marine logistics services.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Accountant

Job Location: Calabar, Cross River State
Employment Type: Full-Time
Industry: Dredging / Marine Operations

Job Summary
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	As part of our growth, we are seeking a smart, detail-oriented, and proactive Accountant to join our dredging operations team in Calabar.
	The ideal candidate will be responsible for managing financial records, monitoring operational expenses, preparing reports, and ensuring accurate accounting processes within the company.


Key Responsibilities


	Maintain accurate financial records and accounting documentation.
	Prepare daily, weekly, and monthly financial reports.
	Monitor cash flow, expenses, and operational costs.
	Process invoices, payments, receipts, and bank reconciliations.
	Manage accounts payable and receivable effectively.
	Assist in payroll preparation and statutory deductions.
	Prepare and post journal entries into accounting systems.
	Support budget preparation and expense tracking for dredging operations.
	Ensure compliance with financial policies, procedures, and regulatory requirements.
	Liaise with auditors, banks, and relevant government agencies when required.
	Maintain proper filing and documentation of financial transactions.
	Provide management with accurate financial analysis and recommendations.


Requirements


	Bachelor’s Degree or HND in Accounting, Finance, or related discipline.
	2–3 years of proven experience in an accounting role.
	Experience in construction, marine, logistics, oil & gas, or dredging industry is an added advantage.
	Strong knowledge of accounting principles and financial reporting.
	Proficiency in accounting software such as QuickBooks, Sage, Tally, Zoho Books, or similar ERP/accounting systems.
	Good knowledge of Microsoft Excel and other Microsoft Office applications.
	Ability to work independently and meet deadlines.
	Strong analytical, organizational, and problem-solving skills.
	High level of integrity, accuracy, and attention to detail.
	ICAN/ACCA certification is an advantage but not compulsory.


Preferred Skills:


	Cost control and operational expense monitoring.
	Inventory and asset tracking experience.
	Ability to prepare management reports and financial summaries.
	Good communication and teamwork skills.


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.

  "description": "EMADOR Nigeria Integrated Resources is a dynamic, multi-sector business dedicated to delivering innovative and reliable solutions across the energy, real estate, and general trading industries. The company specializes in solar energy sales and services, providing sustainable and cost-effective power solutions to homes and businesses.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Medical Records Clerk

Job Location: Ikeja, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Description


	We are looking for a Medical Records Clerk to join our team and oversee the organization and maintenance of patient records in our healthcare facility.


Responsibilities


	Distribute medical charts to the appropriate departments of the hospital
	Maintain accurate records by following hospital procedures
	Ensure patient charts, paperwork, and reports are completed in an accurate and timely manner
	Keep all medical records confidential and protected
	File all patients&rsquo; medical records and information
	Supply the nursing department with the appropriate documents and forms
	Complete clerical duties including answering phones, responding to emails, and processing patient admission and discharge records


Requirements


	Interested candidates should possess an OND / HND with 2 years of experience.
	Advanced understanding of medical terminology and administration processes
	Proficient in information management programs and MS Office
	Outstanding communication and interpersonal abilities
	Strong attention to detail with excellent organizational skills.


Salary
N100,000 - N150,000 monthly.

Method of Application&nbsp;
Intrested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: integratedresourcesemadorniger@gmail.com using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.",

Medical Records Clerk Job at EMADOR Nigeria Integrated Resources




        
    

        
        EMADOR Nigeria Integrated Resources is a dynamic, multi-sector business dedicated to delivering innovative and reliable solutions across the energy, real estate, and general trading industries. The company specializes in solar energy sales and services, providing sustainable and cost-effective power solutions to homes and businesses.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Medical Records Clerk

Job Location: Ikeja, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Description
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	We are looking for a Medical Records Clerk to join our team and oversee the organization and maintenance of patient records in our healthcare facility.


Responsibilities


	Distribute medical charts to the appropriate departments of the hospital
	Maintain accurate records by following hospital procedures
	Ensure patient charts, paperwork, and reports are completed in an accurate and timely manner
	Keep all medical records confidential and protected
	File all patients’ medical records and information
	Supply the nursing department with the appropriate documents and forms
	Complete clerical duties including answering phones, responding to emails, and processing patient admission and discharge records


Requirements


	Interested candidates should possess an OND / HND with 2 years of experience.
	Advanced understanding of medical terminology and administration processes
	Proficient in information management programs and MS Office
	Outstanding communication and interpersonal abilities
	Strong attention to detail with excellent organizational skills.


Salary
N100,000 - N150,000 monthly.

Method of Application 
Intrested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.    

    

        Click here to Apply

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Field Engineer",
  "description": "Cobranet Limited is a reputable organization incorporated in 2003 and provides internet solutions. Our footprint and range of products have grown immensely since incorporation and we are proud to provide every segment of society with a reliable link to the digital universe. Our entire network topology is rugged, dynamic and fully redundant with negligible downtimes: content showing extremely high availability exceeding 99.5%. Our promise is to continue providing innovative solutions at the best feature-to-price and service-to-price ratio.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Field Engineer

Job Location: Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Description
Field Engineer:


	Collaborate with internal teams, such as coordinators, network operations and support, to escalate and resolve complex issues when necessary.
	Maintain detailed documentation of customer interactions, service requests, and problem resolutions.


Customer Service and Communication:


	Interact professionally and courteously with customers, ensuring a positive customer experience during the installation process.
	Communicate technical information to customers in a clear and understandable manner, addressing any questions or concerns they may have.
	Provide recommendations for improving network performance, equipment upgrades, or additional services based on customer requirements and industry best practices.
	Collaborate with sales teams or account managers to gather customer feedback and identify opportunities for upselling or cross-selling services.


Quality Assurance and Compliance:


	Ensure installations are completed according to company standards, policies, and regulatory requirements.
	Perform regular audits and quality checks on installed services to identify any deficiencies and implement corrective actions.
	Stay updated with industry trends, emerging technologies, and best practices related to ISP installations to enhance service delivery and customer satisfaction.


Key Responsibilities
Installation and Configuration:


	Attending to customer premises to install and configure ISP services, including internet connectivity, routers, modems, and other networking equipment.
	Follow installation guidelines, technical specifications, and company procedures to ensure accurate setup and connectivity.
	Test and verify the performance of installed services to ensure they meet the desired speed, reliability, and quality standards.
	Educate customers on the proper use and maintenance of the installed equipment and services.


Troubleshooting and Issue Resolution:


	Diagnose and resolve technical issues related to network connectivity, equipment malfunction, or service disruptions.
	Respond to customer complaints or service requests in a timely manner and provide effective solutions to address their concerns.


Qualification and Skills


	High School Diploma or equivalent; technical certifications or degree in a related field is a plus.
	Must have 5 years of work experience
	Proven experience in network installations, preferably within an ISP or telecommunications environment.
	Strong knowledge of networking concepts, protocols, and equipment, such as routers, switches, modems, and cabling.
	Proficiency in troubleshooting network connectivity issues and resolving technical problems.
	Familiarity with various operating systems (Windows, Linux, macOS) and network diagnostic tools.
	Excellent communication and customer service skills to interact effectively with customers and internal teams.
	Ability to work independently, prioritize tasks, and meet deadlines in a field environment.
	Valid driver&#39;s license and willingness to travel to customer sites as required.
	Physical ability to lift and move networking equipment during installations.


Method of Application&nbsp;
Intrested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: recruitment@cobranet.ng using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.

Note: Only qualiifed candidates will be shortlisted.&nbsp;",
  "datePosted": "2026-06-01",
  "validThrough": "2026-07-01",
  "employmentType": "Full-Time",
  "hiringOrganization": {
    "@type": "Organization",
    "name": "Cobranet Limited"
    
  },
  "jobLocation": {
    "@type": "Place",
    "address": {
      "@type": "PostalAddress",

Field Engineer Job at Cobranet Limited




        
    

        
        Cobranet Limited is a reputable organization incorporated in 2003 and provides internet solutions. Our footprint and range of products have grown immensely since incorporation and we are proud to provide every segment of society with a reliable link to the digital universe. Our entire network topology is rugged, dynamic and fully redundant with negligible downtimes: content showing extremely high availability exceeding 99.5%. Our promise is to continue providing innovative solutions at the best feature-to-price and service-to-price ratio.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Field Engineer

Job Location: Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    



Job Description
Field Engineer:


	Collaborate with internal teams, such as coordinators, network operations and support, to escalate and resolve complex issues when necessary.
	Maintain detailed documentation of customer interactions, service requests, and problem resolutions.


Customer Service and Communication:


	Interact professionally and courteously with customers, ensuring a positive customer experience during the installation process.
	Communicate technical information to customers in a clear and understandable manner, addressing any questions or concerns they may have.
	Provide recommendations for improving network performance, equipment upgrades, or additional services based on customer requirements and industry best practices.
	Collaborate with sales teams or account managers to gather customer feedback and identify opportunities for upselling or cross-selling services.


Quality Assurance and Compliance:


	Ensure installations are completed according to company standards, policies, and regulatory requirements.
	Perform regular audits and quality checks on installed services to identify any deficiencies and implement corrective actions.
	Stay updated with industry trends, emerging technologies, and best practices related to ISP installations to enhance service delivery and customer satisfaction.


Key Responsibilities
Installation and Configuration:


	Attending to customer premises to install and configure ISP services, including internet connectivity, routers, modems, and other networking equipment.
	Follow installation guidelines, technical specifications, and company procedures to ensure accurate setup and connectivity.
	Test and verify the performance of installed services to ensure they meet the desired speed, reliability, and quality standards.
	Educate customers on the proper use and maintenance of the installed equipment and services.


Troubleshooting and Issue Resolution:


	Diagnose and resolve technical issues related to network connectivity, equipment malfunction, or service disruptions.
	Respond to customer complaints or service requests in a timely manner and provide effective solutions to address their concerns.


Qualification and Skills


	High School Diploma or equivalent; technical certifications or degree in a related field is a plus.
	Must have 5 years of work experience
	Proven experience in network installations, preferably within an ISP or telecommunications environment.
	Strong knowledge of networking concepts, protocols, and equipment, such as routers, switches, modems, and cabling.
	Proficiency in troubleshooting network connectivity issues and resolving technical problems.
	Familiarity with various operating systems (Windows, Linux, macOS) and network diagnostic tools.
	Excellent communication and customer service skills to interact effectively with customers and internal teams.
	Ability to work independently, prioritize tasks, and meet deadlines in a field environment.
	Valid driver's license and willingness to travel to customer sites as required.
	Physical ability to lift and move networking equipment during installations.


Method of Application 
Intrested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.

Note: Only qualiifed candidates will be shortlisted.     

    

        Click here to Apply

Fx Sales Account Manager Job at ACCM Global Enterprise Limited




        
    

        
        ACCM Global Enterprise Limited - We are seeking an ambitious and results driven Fx Sales Account Manager with a strong interest in financial markets, trading, and client services. This role offers an excellent opportunity to build a successful career in Forex Brokerage Sales, while contributing to revenue growth and client satisfaction.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Fx Sales Account Manager
Job Location: Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Key Responsibilities
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	Promote and sell company products effectively to prospective and existing clients
	Meet daily call targets and convert leads into successful sales
	Achieve and exceed monthly sales targets and performance objectives
	Deliver exceptional customer experience
	Develop and execute strategic sales plans to expand client base and market share
	Identify and capitalize on new business opportunities, including partnerships with Introducing Brokers
	Analyze market trends, client needs, and competitor activities to improve sales strategies
	Prepare regular sales reports, forecasts, and performance insights
	Collaborate with the marketing team on lead generation and client acquisition strategies
	Conduct market research and provide insights to support trading strategies
	Participate in training programs and continuous professional development
	Support operational efficiency by assisting with back-office functions when required.


Qualifications & Requirements


	Bachelor’s Degree or HND in Finance, Economics, Business Administration, or related fields
	Minimum of 2 years of experience in FX/Forex sales or a similar role
	Proven track record of meeting or exceeding sales targets
	Strong interest in financial markets, trading, and investments
	Familiarity with CRM tools such as Salesforce, Zoho CRM, or HubSpot
	Proficiency in Microsoft Office Suite
	Strong communication, negotiation, and interpersonal skills
	Excellent organizational and time management abilities
	Analytical mindset with strong problem-solving skills
	Familiarity with trading platforms is an added advantage
	Ability to work in a fast-paced, target-driven environment.


What We Offer


	Salary: N300,000 - N350,000 monthly.
	Competitive salary with performance-based incentives
	Health and wellness benefits (HMO)
	A dynamic and collaborative work environment
	Exposure to international financial markets and trading platforms
	Career growth and continuous learning opportunities.


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV and a cover letter to: [email protected] using "Sales Account Manager" as the subject of the email.

Note: If you are passionate about building a career in financial markets and sales, we would love to hear from you.
